title: gumira oblongata
intro: Gumira oblongata, also known as Indian gentian or Gentiana kurroo, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region of India and Tibet, characterized by its unique, elongated shape and vibrant blue flowers. The medicinal properties of Gumira oblongata lie in its high concentration of gentianine and gentiopicrin, compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to stimulate digestion, alleviate symptoms of indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Gumira oblongata is often used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that promote digestive health and boost the immune system.
